All reports are consistent and accurate in their assessments.\n\n- **Scores Justification:**\n  - The malware scores are all 0, which aligns with the analysis; no malicious activity is present.\n  - Obfuscated scores are 0, consistent with the clear, auto-generated nature.\n  - The security risk scores are uniformly low (0.1), reflecting the minimal risk associated with read-only interface code.\n\n- **Potential for Malicious Use:** While the code can be used to connect to a contract and read data, it does not itself perform any malicious actions. The only concern could be misuse—e.g., connecting to a malicious contract—but that is outside the scope of this code's content.\n\n- **Assumptions & Claims:** The reports challenge no assumptions about vulnerabilities; they correctly interpret the code as a benign interface.\n\n### Final Assessment:\n\n- **Malware Score:** 0 (no malicious code or behavior detected)\n- **Obfuscated Score:** 0 (no obfuscation present)\n- **Risk Score:** 0.1 (very low, as it is a standard interface for a public contract)\n\n### My Overall Estimate:\n\nGiven the detailed analysis, I agree with the reports' assessments.
